# meltcloud_elastic_share (Resource)

An Elastic Share grants a consuming organization a slice of an Elastic Capacity, expressed in cores, memory, and disk.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# create cluster and capacity
resource "meltcloud_cluster" "example" {
  name           = "melt02"
  version        = "1.35"
  pod_cidr       = "10.36.0.0/16"
  service_cidr   = "10.96.0.0/16"
  dns_service_ip = "10.96.0.10"
}

resource "meltcloud_elastic_capacity" "example" {
  cluster_id = meltcloud_cluster.example.id
  name       = "capacity1"
}

# allocate a share of the capacity to a consuming organization
resource "meltcloud_elastic_share" "example" {
  capacity_id                 = meltcloud_elastic_capacity.example.id
  consuming_organization_uuid = "deadbeef-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

  name      = "share1"
  cores     = 100
  memory_mb = 102400
  disk_gb   = 1000
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `capacity_id` (Number) ID of the parent Elastic Capacity
- `consuming_organization_uuid` (String) UUID of the consuming Organization
- `cores` (Number) Number of cores granted to the consuming organization
- `disk_gb` (Number) Disk in GB granted to the consuming organization
- `memory_mb` (Number) Memory in MB granted to the consuming organization
- `name` (String) Name of the Elastic Share

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) Internal ID of the Elastic Share on meltcloud

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Resource can be imported by using the resource path as displayed in the URL
terraform import meltcloud_elastic_share.example elastic_shares/1
```
